title: Minimum Cost to Make at Least One Valid Path in a Grid
hints: Treat the grid as a graph where each cell is a node and arrows are edges. Changing an arrow creates a new edge with weight 1.
This is a shortest path problem on a graph with edge weights 0 and 1, which suggests using 0-1 BFS with a deque.
The deque maintains nodes sorted by current minimum cost; process 0-cost moves first to ensure optimal discovery without full Dijkstra overhead.
